title: orobanche ramosa subsp. mutelii
intro: Orobanche ramosa subsp. mutelii, also known as the broomrape, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been found to have potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function, and is often used to treat conditions like arthritis and skin infections. Orobanche ramosa subsp. mutelii can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies to support digestive health and reduce stress.
